items.description: Arabic numeral '13' written in left margin at mid-page height
items.scholarly_note: Post-medieval (likely 19th/20th c.) annotation in Western numerals, distinct from the 13th-c. Gothic book hand. Probably an editorial, stanza, or catalog reference.

items.description: Cluster of Arabic numerals in left margin below the '13', reading approximately '3', '2', '5' with additional '5', '2', '5' or '325'/'525' forms
items.scholarly_note: Modern marginal annotation, possibly a cross-reference to another folio, line number in a printed edition (e.g., line ~325), or catalog collocation mark. Multiple instances suggest repeated reference use.

items.description: Small 'z.' or short curved flourish mark at foot of text block on the left
items.scholarly_note: Unclear function; possible catchword tail, quire signature remnant, or stray modern mark. Ink appears thin; requires further autopsy or multispectral imaging to determine if contemporary with main hand.

items.description: Arabic numeral '30' positioned at top left of the writing area
items.scholarly_note: Foliation or pagination marker. Ink color and placement are consistent with either near-contemporary or early modern addition; common in Icelandic codices for navigation.

items.description: Large ornamental capital with elongated descender beginning the lower section of text
items.scholarly_note: Standard scribal decoration signaling a textual division or new section within the Eddic poem (Reginsmál/Fafnismál). The descender extends downward, typical of the main scribe's mise-en-page practice.

items.description: Brownish age-related discoloration and darkening along outer edge and bottom-right corner of the vellum leaf
items.scholarly_note: Natural patina and minor handling or environmental staining characteristic of calfskin vellum from c. 1270. Text remains fully legible; no active corrosion or ink damage observed.

damage_notes: Vellum exhibits typical medieval Icelandic preparation quality with minor cockling and edge wear.
No holes, perforations, worm damage, tears, or losses visible.
No offset ink, ghosting from facing page, or significant surface abrasion through the text.
Iron-gall ink is stable with normal slight bleeding into the parchment; no modern repairs evident.
summary: Page 59 (Reginsmál/Fafnismál) is a clean, well-preserved leaf with no figurative marginalia, doodles, faces, animals, or pen trials. The only notable non-text additions are modern Arabic numeral annotations in the left margin ('13' and a lower '3 2 5' cluster), a small 'z.' at the foot, and the page '30' at the head. A single large flourished initial with extended descender provides standard textual decoration within the block. The vellum shows normal age-related edge staining and discoloration, especially bottom-right, but has no significant damage, holes, scratches, corrections, or offset ink.
